title: The Letter That Teaches Peace of Heart
usage_rules: Prefer Wikimedia Commons categories and file pages with explicit license/public-domain information.
For modern icons/photos, keep attribution and license in asset_attribution.json.
Do not assume every icon image is free just because the saint is old; verify the file page license.
Use icons reverently: no distorted faces, no mocking zooms, no sensational effects.
Mix icon/portrait assets with monastery exteriors, manuscripts, maps, candles, and landscape footage to avoid a static slideshow.

scenes.title: The Letter Begins at Home
scenes.visual_prompt: Cinematic Orthodox documentary: old Russian letter on a wooden desk, candlelight, portrait of Hegumen Nikon, quiet household doorway, prayer corner.

beats.narration_excerpt: A spiritual letter can sound small from the outside.  No armies, no public miracle, no great council, no golden throne.
beats.asset_note: Scene anchor: use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent recurring visual.

beats.narration_excerpt: Just one elder writing to wounded people and telling them where the battle really begins: at home, with irritation, forgiveness, prayer, and the hidden conditio...

beats.narration_excerpt: Yet in one letter to his spiritual children, he turned their attention to something very near: preserve peace with the people around you, and hurry to ask forgi...

scenes.title: A Priest Formed by Hard Years
scenes.visual_prompt: Russian Orthodox elder portrait, early twentieth-century books, Petrograd student atmosphere, church shadows, restrained Soviet-era documents.

beats.narration_excerpt: Nikon was not writing as a comfortable moralist.  Born Nikolay Vorobiev in 1894 in the Tver region, he searched for truth with unusual seriousness.

beats.narration_excerpt: He studied, read, and pressed into the questions that refuse to leave a person alone: why do I live, what is truth, and what is the meaning of everything?  Orth...

scenes.title: Why Peace Was Not Sentiment
scenes.visual_prompt: Quiet Russian home interior, icon lamp, two chairs separated by shadow, elder portrait watching over a handwritten letter.

beats.narration_excerpt: That history matters because peace, for Nikon, was not a gentle mood for people with easy lives.  Peace was fought for.
beats.asset_note: Scene anchor: use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent recurring visual.
beats.search_query: Orthodox saint icon portrait Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Peace had to be guarded against pride, anger, self-defense, and the wish to win every small conflict.  His letter tells the spiritual children to preserve peace...
beats.asset_note: Use manuscripts, handwritten letters, old books, or generated letter-table closeups.
beats.search_query: peace sentiment guarded against pride anger self Russian manuscript letter book Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: That detail is painfully practical.  It is often easier to speak warmly about mankind than to answer one person in the next room with humility.
beats.asset_note: Use manuscripts, handwritten letters, old books, or generated letter-table closeups.
beats.search_query: peace sentiment detail painfully practical often easier Russian manuscript letter book Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Nikon brings the spiritual life down from abstraction into the place where the heart is actually exposed.

beats.narration_excerpt: Nikon also gives the darker side.  Where peace is absent, darkness and heaviness enter the heart.  Anyone who has lived with resentment knows this.  The mind re...

scenes.title: A Counsel for the Age of Exposure
scenes.visual_prompt: Modern phone screen dimmed beside old prayer book and Nikon portrait, sealed letter, candlelight, restrained Orthodox documentary mood.

beats.narration_excerpt: This letter feels unexpectedly modern.  We live in a world that rewards instant reaction, public anger, and the careful performance of being right.  Nikon's cou...
beats.asset_note: Scene anchor: use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent recurring visual.

beats.narration_excerpt: Preserve peace.  Seek reconciliation without delay.  Appeal to the name of Jesus Christ as often as possible.  Pour out your troubles before the Lord.

beats.narration_excerpt: Act according to conscience.  He is not teaching a technique for looking spiritual.  He is teaching the slow recovery of an undivided heart.

scenes.title: Not Self-Help, Repentance
scenes.visual_prompt: Old confessional mood, prayer book, bowed silhouette, elder portrait, text overlay space: repentance, mercy, peace.

beats.narration_excerpt: It would be easy to turn this into self-help: five steps to inner peace.  Nikon gives us something harder and more merciful.  The problem is not only stress.
beats.asset_note: Scene anchor: use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent recurring visual.
beats.search_query: Orthodox saint icon portrait Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: It is sin, self-justification, spiritual blindness, and pride.  The cure is not self-improvement as an identity.  It is repentance before God.
beats.asset_note: Use manuscripts, handwritten letters, old books, or generated letter-table closeups.
beats.search_query: self help repentance justification spiritual blindness pride Russian manuscript letter book Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: That is why the letter begins with seeing our sins and asking the Lord to reveal what we hide even from ourselves.  Peace of heart is not escape from truth.  It...
beats.asset_note: Use manuscripts, handwritten letters, old books, or generated letter-table closeups.
beats.search_query: self help repentance letter begins seeing sins Russian manuscript letter book Wikimedia Commons

scenes.title: When Peace Feels Impossible
scenes.visual_prompt: Sober Orthodox counseling mood, church candle stand, closed letter, elder portrait, no sensational imagery, reverent restraint.

beats.narration_excerpt: There is an important caution here.  Nikon is not saying that every wound is simple, or that every dangerous situation should be endured without help.

beats.narration_excerpt: It calls us to reconciliation where reconciliation is possible, and to prayer where it is not yet possible.  Sometimes the first step is not a dramatic embrace.

beats.narration_excerpt: Sometimes it is to stop cursing the other person inwardly, to ask God for mercy, and to seek wise counsel from the Church.
